mnemonics: ARIMA = Auto-Regressive (AR) my past, Integrated (I) my differences, Moving Average (MA) my errors
keyPoints: ARIMA models combine autoregression (AR), differencing to make a series stationary (I), and a moving average component (MA) to forecast future values.
The 'I' or Integrated component refers to the order of differencing (d) needed to achieve stationarity in the time series.
The model is denoted as ARIMA(p,d,q), where p is the AR order, d is the differencing order, and q is the MA order.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for stationarity before fitting an ARIMA model, leading to spurious results.
Over-differencing the series, which can introduce unnecessary correlation and weaken the model.
